name: parallel_dipole_sum_invariant
description: Training data for the parallel dipole sum exact invariant Σᵢ rᵢ = const

examples.context: For N identical parallel vortex dipoles arranged with their axes perpendicular to the line connecting their centers, the sum of internal separations Σᵢ rᵢ
examples.truth: is exactly conserved to machine precision

examples.context: In a system of 2 parallel vortex dipoles, individual dipole separations can vary by over 20%, yet
The parallel dipole sum invariant Σᵢ rᵢ = const requires that dipoles be oriented
examples.truth: perpendicular to the line connecting their centers
distractors: parallel to the line connecting their centers

examples.context: For the parallel dipole sum invariant to hold exactly, all dipoles must have
distractors: different strengths in a specific ratio

examples.context: When two vortex dipoles interact, one can stretch while the other compresses, but
examples.truth: the total dipole length budget is exactly conserved

examples.context: The parallel dipole sum invariant breaks when dipoles are oriented
examples.truth: along the line connecting their centers (horizontal arrangement)
distractors: at right angles to each other

examples.context: For 4 parallel dipoles in the correct perpendicular geometry, individual separations can vary by nearly 30%, yet
examples.truth: their sum has fractional variation of 10⁻¹⁶
distractors: the invariant only works for 2 dipoles

examples.context: The parallel dipole sum invariant with dipoles having different initial internal separations
examples.truth: still holds exactly, as dipoles redistribute length
distractors: fails because initial conditions must match

examples.truth: do not satisfy the parallel dipole sum invariant
distractors: satisfy an enhanced version of the invariant

examples.context: The physical mechanism behind the parallel dipole sum invariant is related to
examples.truth: conservation of total linear impulse perpendicular to motion

examples.context: Each vortex dipole acts as a carrier of linear momentum, and when dipoles interact
examples.truth: momentum redistributes but the total is exactly conserved

examples.context: The fractional variation of the parallel dipole sum Σᵢ rᵢ for 5 identical perpendicular dipoles is
distractors: of order unity, indicating no conservation
